items.title: Trump blasts New York AI data center moratorium, says state should change policy 'immediately'
items.description: New York became the first state in the U.S. to impose a ban of its kind when the governor signed the executive order Tuesday.

items.title: SpaceX stock sinks below $135 IPO price for the first time
items.description: SpaceX shares fell for a fourth-straight session on Wednesday days after entering the Nasdaq-100

items.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
items.description: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

items.title: Trump's pick to head national intelligence, Jay Clayton, won't tell senators Biden won the 2020 election
items.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee is considering Jay Clayton's nomination to be the director of national intelligence.

items.title: J&J falls despite a beat-and-raise quarter. Why we're raising our price target anyways
items.description: Johnson & Johnson's quarter was imperfect, but enough is going right to validate our ownership of the stock.

items.title: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's testimony to Senate banking committee hits on economy, interest rates
items.description: Part of congressionally mandated Capitol Hill appearances for the central bank leader, Warsh spoke Tuesday to the House Financial Services Committee.

items.title: Todd Blanche says he has 'full faith' in Kash Patel as FBI director
items.description: Attorney General nominee Todd Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Donald Trump.

items.title: Hassett sees no 'excuse' to raise rates, says Warsh will push Fed to 'right answer'
items.description: Hassett, on CNBC, credited President Donald Trump's policies after the latest consumer price index reading came in much lower than expected.

items.title: Amazon senior cloud executive departs after 18 years
items.description: Brown helped launch one of AWS' oldest services and also oversaw its compute and machine learning units.

items.title: Public Service Loan Forgiveness has new rules — 3 changes borrowers should know about
items.description: Recent changes to Public Service Loan Forgiveness should prompt borrowers to confirm that their repayment plan and loan type remain eligible for the relief.

items.title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
items.description: Anthropic is lining up investor meetings ahead of a potential IPO as soon as October as the AI startup behind looks to beat rival OpenAI to the public markets.

items.title: Historic IBM stock crash sets up unique options strategy
items.description: IBM shares fell just over $73 to ~$217 — a jaw-dropping 25% single-day decapitation.

items.title: Fed Chairman Warsh says he meets 'often' with Trump administration, defends independence
items.description: Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that he speaks with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent frequently outside their regular weekly meetings.

items.title: Warren Buffett tells CNBC he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's investment in Alphabet
items.description: Warren Buffett said he was the driving force behind the conglomerate's recent big investment in Alphabet.

items.title: ASML shares fall after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
items.description: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

items.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano will lead Trump Accounts expansion
items.description: The Treasury Department is putting a top official in charge of the new program as it enrolls millions of families.

items.title: Warren Buffett on the market today: 'It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling'
items.description: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

items.title: Warren Buffett calls Bill Gates' actions with Epstein 'distasteful' but says people make mistakes
items.description: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman left out the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts for the first time in 20 years.

items.title: DTCC, Wall Street’s post-trade powerhouse, tests tokenized markets with industry heavy hitters
items.description: The DTCC is pushing to bring tokenization of real world assets from concept to reality using existing financial infrastructure.

items.title: Goldman Sachs’ former top lawyer tells House Epstein was a ‘masterful liar’ who used her to bolster his standing
items.description: Kathryn Ruemmler said earlier this year she would leave Goldman Sachs at the end of June after fallout over her emails with Jeffrey Epstein.
